title: Garment source geometry hint v1 (separate shell) vs v6 material regions — peds school-age patient
subtitle: Model Vetting Studio front/three-quarter/body_motion evidence for procedural separate-shell garmentSourceGeometryHint delta (Q1 factory)
objectives: Confirm garmentSourceGeometryHint fields in just-produced rigging_report for peds_asthma_parent_anxiety_v1 patient (mode=separate_shell_source_geometry_hint_v1, hasVisibleVolume=true, hasSeamFoldHints=true, faceCount=40, parented, claimScope preserved).

Pack Model Vetting Studio evidence (front, three-quarter, body_motion captures) demonstrating visible separate shell geometry (volume via SOLIDIFY 0.011, radial ripple, hem/collar bands, parented to body) vs v6 baseline (bounds-based material regions on native mesh only, no detached shell).
Verify the hint object follows root motion in body_motion_probe (asthma breathing effort clip) without detachment; record as procedural source hint only.
Keep all promotion gates false; advance Q1 case phenotype+garmentLayers -> generated separate shell in report+GLB.
processSteps: Run garment_source_geometry_hint=true through orchestrate/automate_blender for peds patient in peds_asthma_parent_anxiety_v1 (fixed skinning depth/center calc).
Produce GLB + rigging_report with garmentSourceGeometryHint block + separate 'openclinxr_garment_hint_peds_tshirt_v1' object (40 faces post-SOLIDIFY/WEIGHTED_NORMAL).
Register GLB path mapping in Model Vetting Studio candidate-capture glbUrlForPath; build/pack this cagematch report page with delta objectives and lookFor for studio captures.
Capture front/three-quarter (fixed) + body_motion_probe (live source skinning or fallback on asthma clip) side-by-side or single in studio; save pngs to this report dir for registry thumbnail.
Adversarial review (productivity-skeptic, visual-realism-adversary) of captures for visible shell delta vs v6 material-only before next bind or UI-XR consumer slice.

caseContext.actorProfile: school-age pediatric asthma patient (Maya Johnson, slender_asthma, garmentLayers=short_sleeve_exam_tshirt)

technologies.displayName: Anny forward + Blender procedural separate-shell garmentSourceGeometryHint v1
technologies.summary: Case-driven (phenotype + garmentLayers) produces minimal 40-face cylindrical shell with ripple/bulge/hem/collar bands + solidify thickness for visible volume; parented for root motion; recorded in rigging_report.garmentSourceGeometryHint.
strengths: Separate shell (not material region) provides explicit source geometry for future real garment topology cagematch or retarget.
Visible volume + seam/fold hints in isolated Model Vetting front/three-quarter/body_motion views.
Parented to body ensures motion follow in breathing clip without full weights.
Preserves notEvidenceFor and claimScope; v1 only for peds_school_age in this case.
limitations: 40-face procedural hint, not production wardrobe or licensed garment topology.
No vertex weights yet (v1 parent only); full skin later.
Still B-grade fixture-grade overall; delta vs v6 is the addition of separate object.

technologies.displayName: v6 bounds-based native mesh material regions (no separate shell)
technologies.summary: Prior clothing implementation assigned top/lower/trim materials directly to native Anny body faces via bounds (wider trim in v6); no detached geometry object.
strengths: Mesh-native, zero extra faces for clothing.
Sufficient for basic role colorway in runtime.
limitations: No visible volume, no separate shell for source hint or cagematch against real garments.
Jagged seam read on low-poly peds; clothing read as painted panels not layered shell.

feasibilityCriteria.label: Visible separate shell geometry delta
feasibilityCriteria.question: Does the output produce a distinct parented shell object with volume/ripple/seams visible in Model Vetting captures, vs v6 material-only?

anny_parametric_forward_pass_plus_blende.note: garmentSourceGeometryHint present with hasVisibleVolume/hasSeamFoldHints; 40-face shell + SOLIDIFY in GLB.

v6_garment_source_quality_material_regio.note: Only material regions on body mesh; no separate object or volume geometry.

feasibilityCriteria.label: Parented follow in body motion
feasibilityCriteria.question: Does the shell geometry follow the body root in body_motion_probe without detachment or drift?

anny_parametric_forward_pass_plus_blende.note: garment.parent = body mesh_obj; selected asthma breathing clip drives root; captureModel/model sync in studio.

feasibilityCriteria.label: Rigging report contract + provenance
feasibilityCriteria.question: Does rigging_report + bundle + provenance record the hint fields truthfully with notEvidenceFor?

anny_parametric_forward_pass_plus_blende.note: Full garmentSourceGeometryHint block in peds_patient_child_rigging_report.json; bundle/provenance sidecars present; claimScope procedural only.

feasibilityCriteria.label: Local reproducible (no cloud/paid)
feasibilityCriteria.question: Can the garment hint path be re-run locally on M1 Max inside approved boundaries?

anny_parametric_forward_pass_plus_blende.note: tools/openclinxr/asset-pipeline/anny/* + local Anny package + Blender background; garment_source_geometry_hint flag.

v6_garment_source_quality_material_regio.note: Prior v6 material pass was local.

feasibilityCriteria.label: Model Vetting Studio capture delta
feasibilityCriteria.question: Do front/three-quarter/body_motion captures clearly show the separate shell (volume, folds, parent) vs v6 baseline?

anny_parametric_forward_pass_plus_blende.note: Studio renderCandidateCapture + body_motion_probe will expose the openclinxr_garment_hint_peds_tshirt_v1 object as distinct blue shell with thickness/ripples outside body surface.

v6_garment_source_quality_material_regio.note: No shell object; clothing read only as material color bands on body.

decisionBranches.condition: School-age peds patient case with garmentLayers needing explicit separate source geometry hint for future wardrobe cagematch (not material paint)
decisionBranches.rationale: Produces parented, SOLIDIFY'd, rippled shell with hasVisibleVolume/seamFoldHints recorded in report; enables delta review vs prior v6 material-only.
exampleScenarios: future peds cases with short_sleeve_exam_tshirt or similar

decisionBranches.condition: Runtime only needs role colorway without separate geometry for cagematch or retarget
decisionBranches.rationale: Lower face count, mesh-native; sufficient when no source-hint requirement.
exampleScenarios: Quick internal fixtures before hint path stabilized

interimVerdict.summary: Retain garment hint v1 separate-shell path as the Q1 factory advance for peds case garmentLayers. v6 material regions remain as baseline for comparison. Visible delta (shell volume/parenting) must be captured in Model Vetting Studio front/three-quarter/body_motion before UI-XR bind or further asset slices. All gates remain blocked.

media.label: Front capture — garment hint shell delta
media.caption: Isolated Model Vetting Studio front view of peds patient with garment hint v1 separate shell (left/primary) vs v6 material-regions baseline (right). Look for distinct blue shell offset from body with visible thickness (SOLIDIFY), torso ripple, hem/collar bands.
lookFor: Left (hint v1): separate 'openclinxr_garment_hint_peds_tshirt_v1' object with radial volume outside body surface, not painted-on.
Collar band and ripple geometry visible at neck/hem.
Right (v6): clothing read only as material color change on native body faces; no offset shell.
Both under same phenotype (slender_asthma, soft_blue tshirt); no props or scene.

media.label: Three-quarter capture — shell volume and seams
media.caption: Three-quarter angle in Model Vetting Studio highlighting silhouette delta and seam/fold hints on the separate shell.
lookFor: Hint shell shows 3D layering (thickness + bulge) vs flat v6 material bands.
Seam/fold geometry (collar + ripple) readable at angle; not present on v6 baseline.
Child proportions and asthma build preserved on both.
No hair/eyes/face detail as differentiator here — focus on garment source geometry.

media.label: Body motion probe — parented shell follow
media.caption: Body_motion_probe capture (asthma breathing effort clip) in studio; shell must translate/rotate with body root via parent, no lag or detachment.
lookFor: During breathing cycle: hint shell (volume+ripples) moves rigidly with chest/spine as parented child of body mesh.
No floating or skinning artifacts on the 40-face shell (v1 parent-only).
v6 material regions deform with native skin weights as expected.
Clip selection via selectBodyMotionProbeClipName (role_patient_asthma_breathing_effort present in GLB).
Bounds probe sane; source surface visible.

processExplanations.title: Load garment hint candidate in Model Vetting Studio
processExplanations.narrative: Use studio with captureCandidateId for the garment-hint glb (now mapped in glbUrlForPath) or cagematchReport=peds-garment-hint-separate-shell-v1-vs-v6-2026-06-07. Trigger front, three_quarter, body_motion_probe views via renderCandidateCapture.
lookFor: GLB loads with the openclinxr_garment_hint_peds_tshirt_v1 mesh present (meshCount includes it).
For body_motion: live source skinning or associated envelope; asthma clip drives parent transform.
Evidence object window.__openClinXrModelVettingCandidateCaptureEvidence has bodyMotionProbePresent true.

processExplanations.title: Inspect separate shell vs v6 delta
processExplanations.narrative: Compare captures: hint shell should appear as distinct offset geometry (blue tshirt material) with thickness and procedural folds; v6 as surface material assignment only.
lookFor: hasVisibleVolume and hasSeamFoldHints from report match visual: volume from SOLIDIFY, ripples from sin(), bands from collar geo.
Parent relationship visible in motion (shell does not lag body root).
Face count 40 on hint object; no extra weights yet.

processExplanations.title: Record and publish evidence
processExplanations.narrative: Save studio canvas screenshots as front.png / three_quarter.png / body_motion.png (or video for motion) into this report dir; update registry. Re-run pnpm asset:cagematch:report-page if tooling exists.
lookFor: Report media entries point to the saved pngs under /cagematch-reports/peds-garment-hint-.../
All notEvidenceFor and claimScope strings preserved in report and rigging sidecar.

captureEvidence.producedBy: tools/openclinxr/evidence/model-vetting-turntable-capture.ts (run with --source-report on packed cagematch report + --capture-views front,three-quarter,body_motion_probe --output-dir .openclinxr/evidence/peds-garment-hint-separate-shell-v1-2026-06-07; orchestrate --garment-source-geometry-hint not re-run (GLB/rigging_report already per prior; verified via source)
captureEvidence.verifiedGarmentSourceGeometryHint: automate_blender.py:1139 (def create_garment_source_geometry_hint) + :1226 return {mode: 'separate_shell_source_geometry_hint_v1', hasVisibleVolume: true, hasSeamFoldHints: true, faceCount: 40 (5x8+collar bands), parentedTo: 'body_mesh_for_root_motion_v1', objectName: 'openclinxr_garment_hint_peds_tshirt_v1', SOLIDIFY 0.011 + WEIGHTED_NORMAL, claimScope/notEvidenceFor preserved}; orchestrate_character.py:386 (if garment_source_geometry_hint flag), :463 (arg), :1414 (call in blender stage); rigging_report assembly :1518
captureEvidence.deltaVsV6: separate shell (volume+parent+ripples+bands) vs material-regions-only; parent follow in body_motion; 40-face procedural hint only
captureEvidence.mirrorNote: cagematch report dir + registry updated; session images are source evidence (mirror to public/cagematch/.../*.png|webm per policy if delta on next asset sync; report media urls remain for studio /cagematch-reports resolution)
